ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00GJ covers cooked chicken meat broadly; no specific code for skinless deep-fried drumstick exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for skinless fried chicken drumstick without risk of fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium skinless fried chicken drumstick weighs approximately 85 g after cooking and skin removal.

nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary macros (energy 195 kcal, protein 28.62 g, fat 8.08 g, carbohydrate 0 g, sodium 96 mg, potassium 249 mg, calcium 12 mg, iron 1.32 mg, cholesterol 94 mg) are taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F11010038 (confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed), which is an approved primary regional source. Saturated fat (2.18 g), monounsaturated fat (3.02 g), polyunsaturated fat (1.79 g), and trans fat (0.1 g estimated) are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05167 (Chicken, drumstick, meat only, cooked, fried) as the closest approved-source equivalent for fatty acid fractions not reported in the HPB record. Both HPB and USDA are approved sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05167 and HPB Foodo F11010038
gi_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central; Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore
gi_source.source_match_notes: Fried chicken drumstick without skin contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g as confirmed by both HPB Foodo F11010038 and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05167. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. Per Project Veritas v2 rules for negligible-carbohydrate foods, glycemic_index is set to null (not 0) and gi_category is set to not_applicable.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (96 mg/100 g) with favourable potassium ratio (249 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 96 mg/100 g is well below the 200 mg threshold for a low-sodium food, and the sodium-to-potassium ratio is favourable; however, patients should avoid adding salt-based marinades or high-sodium dipping sauces, and should monitor total cholesterol intake given 94 mg cholesterol per 100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and high protein (28.62 g/100 g) with no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g, this food produces no postprandial glucose excursion; the high protein content (28.62 g/100 g) supports satiety and glycaemic stability, making it a suitable protein source for individuals with type 2 diabetes when prepared without sugary marinades or glazes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol 94 mg/100 g and saturated fat 2.18 g/100 g from deep-frying; trans fat estimated at 0.1 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The deep-frying process introduces saturated fat (2.18 g/100 g) and a small estimated trans fat fraction (0.1 g/100 g), both of which are atherogenic; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 85 g per serving, choose unsaturated cooking oils, and consider oven-baking as a lower-fat alternative to reduce LDL-raising lipid exposure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried chicken drumstick without skin is a cooked dark-meat poultry product prepared from the lower leg portion of the chicken. The skin is removed before or after deep-frying, yielding a leaner product compared to skin-on fried chicken. The result is a tender, juicy piece of dark meat with a lightly crisped exterior and rich, savoury flavour derived from the Maillard reaction during frying. Per 100 g, it provides 195 kcal, 28.62 g protein, 8.08 g fat, and 0 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate food suitable for low-carbohydrate and ketogenic dietary patterns. Sodium is low at 96 mg/100 g and potassium is 249 mg/100 g, giving a favourable electrolyte profile for blood pressure management. Cholesterol is moderately elevated at 94 mg/100 g, which is relevant for cardiovascular risk assessment. The absence of carbohydrate means glycaemic index is not applicable. This food is appropriate for individuals with type 2 diabetes seeking protein-rich, carbohydrate-free options, and for hypertension management when consumed without added salt. The frying method generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and a small estimated trans fat fraction, which are pro-inflammatory considerations relevant to rheumatoid arthritis and cardiovascular disease. It is classified as halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
